XOnlineOfferingCancel

Creates an asynchronous task to cancel a specified online offering owned by a specified user.

HRESULT XOnlineOfferingCancel(
    DWORD dwUserIndex,
    XOFFERING_ID OfferingId,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phTask
);

Parameters

dwUserIndex
[in] Controller index (zero-based) of the user who will be canceling the offering. The user must have been successfully logged on with a previous call to XOnlineLogon.
OfferingId
[in] Unique identifier associated with the offering to be canceled. This identifier is obtained from an XONLINEOFFERING_INFO structure when offerings are enumerated.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle to an event that will be signaled when there is work to be done on the cancel task. This parameter can be NULL if no such event is required.
phTask
[out] Address of an XONLINETASK_HANDLE that receives the offering cancel task. This task sends the cancellation request to the offering service and receives the response. This handle must be pumped to completion by XOnlineTaskContinue. After the task has completed, call XOnlineTaskClose to close the task.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful.

Remarks

XOnlineOfferingCancel returns an asynchronous task which must be pumped with XOnlineTaskContinue until that function returns XONLINETASK_S_SUCCESS, indicating that the cancel request has completed, or until an error code is returned, indicating that the cancel has failed. The task handle should then be closed with XOnlineTaskClose. Calling XOnlineTaskClose before the task completes or indicates an error will not succeed in closing the task.

Among the error codes that XOnlineTaskContinue may return for the cancel task are those that indicate specific billing or provider errors, such as the specified offering being non-cancelable or nonexistent. These errors code are defined in the xonline.h header, and are in the XONLINE_E_OFFERING family.

XOnlineOfferingCancel cancels an online offering that has been previously purchased by the specified user. The offering must be cancelable, which is usually indicative of an offering with a recurring charge. Free offers cannot be canceled. If the user owns multiple instances of the specified offering, an arbitrary one will be canceled.

Note    The instance count returned by XOnlineOfferingDetails may not reflect offerings that have been recently (within the last few seconds) canceled by the XOnlineOfferingCancel function. Offering instance counts are decremented asynchronously, not immediately upon success of an XOnlineOfferingCancel operation. XOnlineOfferingDetails should not be used to verify instance counts immediately following offering cancellation. The success of the XOnlineOfferingCancel operation itself is sufficient guarantee that the instant count will be subsequently decremented. Also note that this applies to premium subscriptions only, not to content (because content is not cancelable).

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineOfferingPurchase, XRemoveContent, XOnlineTaskContinue, XOnlineTaskClose
